modules.name: gb_code
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
This files marks the containing directory as Python package.

modules.name: gb_code.gb_generator
documentation:
This module produces GB structures. You need to run csl_generator first
to get the info necessary for your grain boundary of interest.

 The code runs in one mode only and takes all the necessary options to write
 a final GB structure from the input io_file, which is written  after you run
 csl_generator.py. You must customize the io_file that comes with some default
 values. For ex.: input the GB_plane of interest from  running the
 CSl_generator in the second mode. Once you have completed customizing the
 io_file, run:

 'gb_generator.py io_file'
!/usr/bin/env python

classes.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.ParseGB
documentation:
parses the GB input axis, basis, lattice parameter,
m and n integers and gb plane.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.WriteGB
documentation:
parses the arguments overlap distance, dimensions, rigid body translate
and file type and writes the final structure to the file.
Possible keys:
    (whichG, a, b)

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.CSL_Ortho_unitcell_atom_generator
documentation: populates a unitcell from the orthogonal vectors.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.CSL_Bicrystal_Atom_generator
documentation: builds the unitcells for both grains g1 and g2.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.Expand_Super_cell
documentation: expands the smallest CSL unitcell to the given dimensions.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.Find_overlapping_Atoms
documentation: finds the overlapping atoms.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.Translate
documentation:
translates the GB on a mesh created by a, b integers and writes
to LAMMPS or VASP.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.Write_to_Vasp
documentation: write a single GB without translations to POSCAR.

methods.name: gb_code.gb_generator.GB_character.Write_to_Lammps
documentation: write a single GB without translations to LAMMPS.
a grain boundary class, encompassing all the characteristics of a GB.

modules.name: gb_code.csl_generator
documentation:
This module is a collection of functions that produce CSL properties.
When run from the terminal, the code runs in two modes.

 First mode:
  'csl_generator.py u v w [limit]' ----->  Where the u v w are the
  indices of the rotation axis such as 1 0 0, 1 1 1, 1 1 0 and so on. The limit
  is the maximum Sigma of interest.
  (the limit by default: 100)

 Second mode:
 'csl_generator.py u v w basis sigma [limit]' -----> Where basis is
  either fcc, bcc, diamond or sc. You read the sigma of interest from the first
  mode run. The limit here refers to CSL GB inclinations. The bigger the limit,
  the higher the indices of CSL planes.
  (the limit by default: 2)

  your chosen axis, basis and sigma will be written to an io_file which will
  later be read by gb_geberator.py.
!/usr/bin/env python

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.get_cubic_sigma
documentation:
CSL analytical formula based on the book:
'Interfaces in crystalline materials',
 Sutton and Balluffi, clarendon press, 1996.
 generates possible sigma values.
arguments:
uvw -- the axis
m,n -- two integers (n by default 1)

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.get_cubic_theta
documentation:
generates possible theta values.
arguments:
uvw -- the axis
m,n -- two integers (n by default 1)

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.get_theta_m_n_list
documentation: Finds integers m and n lists that match the input sigma.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.print_list
documentation: prints a list of smallest sigmas/angles for a given axis(uvw).

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.rot
documentation:
produces a rotation matrix.
arguments:
a -- an axis
Theta -- an angle

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.integer_array
documentation: returns True if an array is ineteger.
Helpful Functions:

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.angv
documentation: returns the angle between two vectors.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.ang
documentation: returns the cos(angle) between two vectors.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.CommonDivisor
documentation: returns the common factor of vector a and the reduced vector.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.SmallestInteger
documentation: returns the smallest multiple integer to make an integer array.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.integerMatrix
documentation: returns an integer matrix from row vectors.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.SymmEquivalent
documentation: returns cubic symmetric eqivalents of the given 2 dimensional vector.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Tilt_Twist_comp
documentation:
returns the tilt and twist components of a given GB plane.
arguments:
v1 -- given gb plane
uvw -- axis of rotation
m,n -- the two necessary integers

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Create_Possible_GB_Plane_List
documentation:
generates GB planes and specifies the character.

arguments:
uvw -- axis of rotation.
m,n -- the two necessary integers
lim -- upper limit for the plane indices

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Create_minimal_cell_Method_1
documentation:
finds Minimal cell by means of a numerical search.
(An alternative analytical method can be used too).
arguments:
sigma -- gb sigma
uvw -- rotation axis
R -- rotation matrix

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Basis
documentation: defines the basis.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Find_Orthogonal_cell
documentation:
finds Orthogonal cells from the CSL minimal cells.
arguments:
basis -- lattice basis
uvw -- rotation axis
m,n -- two necessary integers
GB1 -- input plane orientation

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.print_list_GB_Planes
documentation: prints lists of GB planes given an axis, basis, m and n.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.odd_even
documentation: finds odd and even elements of a matrix.
___CSL/DSC vector construction___#
According to Grimmer et al. (Acta Cryst. (1974). A30, 197-207) ,
DSC and CSL lattices for bcc and fcc were made from the Sc lattice
via body_centering and face_centering:

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.body_centering
documentation: converting a single crystal minimal cell to a bcc one.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.face_centering
documentation: converting a single crystal minimal cell to an fcc one.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.DSC_vec
documentation:
a discrete shift complete (DSC)
network for the given sigma and minimal cell.
arguments:
basis -- a lattice basis(fcc or bcc)
sigma -- gb sigma
minicell -- gb minimal cell

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.CSL_vec
documentation:
CSL minimal cell for sc, fcc and bcc.
arguments:
basis -- a lattice basis(fcc or bcc)
minicell -- gb minimal cell

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.DSC_on_plane
documentation: projects the given DSC network on a given plane.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.CSL_density
documentation: returns the CSL density of a given plane and its d_spacing.

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Ortho_fcc_bcc
documentation: An auxilary function to help reduce the size of the small orthogonal cell
that is decided otherwise based on Sc for fcc and bcc

functions.name: gb_code.csl_generator.Write_to_io
documentation:
an input file for gb_generator.py that can be customized.
It also contains the output from the usage of csl_generator.py.
Writing to a yaml file that will be read by gb_generator
